title: ChatGPT Unlocked: Your AI Sidekick

modules.title: What Just Talked Back to Me?
modules.sub: Discover what ChatGPT actually is under the hood
modules.description: You've just opened ChatGPT for the first time and it responds — but what exactly is doing the talking? In this module you explore how large language models predict and generate text, busting the myth that AI truly 'understands' you. You'll make choices in the story that reveal the difference between a thinking brain and a very clever pattern-matcher.

modules.title: Prompts That Actually Work
modules.sub: Learn to ask AI questions that get useful answers
modules.description: Your project deadline is tomorrow and vague questions are getting you nowhere fast. You'll experiment with rewriting weak prompts into clear, specific ones and immediately see how the quality of ChatGPT's response changes. By the end, you'll have a personal formula for crafting prompts that get you what you actually need.

modules.title: When ChatGPT Gets It Wrong
modules.sub: Spot AI mistakes before they become your mistakes
modules.description: ChatGPT just handed you a confident-sounding answer that turns out to be completely false — now what? You'll practise fact-checking AI outputs and learn why the tool sometimes 'hallucinates' sources, dates, and details. You'll leave this module with a quick checklist for deciding when to trust, verify, or ditch an AI response.

modules.title: Build Your Own AI Cheat Sheet
modules.sub: Create a personal reference guide for using ChatGPT well
modules.description: You pull together everything you've learned by designing your own one-page AI cheat sheet you'd actually keep. You'll choose which prompt tips, warning signs, and use-case rules matter most to you and arrange them in a format that makes sense to your brain. The finished sheet is yours to keep — a ready-to-use tool for every future project.

synopsis: Step inside a story where you're a student who just discovered ChatGPT the night before a big project — and every choice you make reveals how the tool really works. You'll leave knowing how to talk to AI, spot its mistakes, and use it in ways that actually help you.
rationale: Millions of middle schoolers are already using ChatGPT with no guidance on how it works, what it can't do, or how to get useful results — making this the highest-priority foundational gap in youth AI literacy.
learning_outcome: Students can write effective prompts, identify unreliable outputs, and explain in their own words what ChatGPT is and is not doing when it responds.
source_signals: 26% of students ages 13-17 are using ChatGPT to help with homework, study finds
Tali Horowitz: How Teens Are Really Using AI
How Students Can Create Notes using BlackBox AI! #shorts #chatgpt #ai #ani #ytshorts
